content.plain_english: This week in Year 2 maths, your child is working on Position and direction. The learning focuses on: use mathematical vocabulary to describe position, direction and movement, including:; movement in a straight line; and rotation as a turn. These skills build directly on what they have already mastered and prepare them for the next steps in the programme of study.
content.in_school: Children handle, sort, and classify 2-D and 3-D shapes, drawing and describing their properties. They use protractors, mirrors, and squared paper to explore angles, symmetry, and position. Explaining what they notice in precise mathematical language is a key part of every lesson.
content.home_activity: Look for real-life examples around the house — a map showing distances, a measuring jug with ml and pints, a food label in grams and kg. Ask your child to read the measurements and convert between them. Road signs in miles are another good context.
dinner_table_questions: Can you show me how to use mathematical vocabulary to describe position, direction and movement, including:?
Was it easy or tricky to movement in a straight line? Why?
How would you explain today's maths to someone who has never seen it before?

key_vocabulary.definition: A pair of numbers giving the exact position of a point on a grid — e.g. (3, 4).
The horizontal line on a coordinate grid.
The vertical line on a coordinate grid.
Sliding a shape to a new position without rotating or flipping it.
Flipping a shape over a mirror line.

example_questions: Write three hundred and twelve in numerals.
content.times_table_tip: Year 2 children learn their 2, 5, 10, and 3 times tables. The 3 times table is the new one this year. Try chanting together: 3, 6, 9, 12, 15 … Clapping a beat as you say the numbers helps the rhythm stick.
